title: Heptapleurum tanytrichum
intro: Heptapleurum tanytrichum, also known as the Pacific polypody, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of infections. Bioactive constituents of Heptapleurum tanytrichum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which have been reported to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.

The classification of Heptapleurum tanytrichum is further defined by its order (Apiales) and class (Magnoliopsida).
